description:
You are a Linux systems engineer performing a quick‐and‐dirty hardening assessment of authentication events.  
A sample log file has already been supplied for you at:

    /home/user/work/logs/auth.log

Your goal is to create a tiny, reproducible “evidence bundle” by filtering the log with regular expressions and
generating a short summary.  Everything you create must live under the new directory:

    /home/user/work/filtered

The bundle must contain exactly three artefacts:

1. /home/user/work/filtered/failed_root.log  
   • Contains only the original auth.log lines that match *both* of the following conditions:  
     a) The line shows a failed SSH password authentication attempt (case-sensitive substring “Failed password”).  
     b) The line targets user “root” (exact word match).  
   • Preserve each matched line verbatim, in its original order.  
   • Do not add blank lines or headers.

2. /home/user/work/filtered/accepted_root.log  
   • Same requirements as #1, but for successful root logins.  
     a) Match the case-sensitive substring “Accepted password”.  
     b) User must be “root”.  
   • Preserve lines verbatim and in order; no blank lines or headers.

3. /home/user/work/filtered/summary.txt  
   • A two-line text file with the following fixed format (each keyword in ALL-CAPS followed by a colon, a single
     space, then an integer count, no extra spaces or trailing characters):

        FAILED_ROOT: <number>
        ACCEPTED_ROOT: <number>

     where <number> is the exact count of lines written to the corresponding *.log file.

Your tasks (5-10 shell commands total):

• Create the /home/user/work/filtered directory (create parents if necessary).  
• Use a single appropriate regular expression for each filter so that the command line itself documents the
  logic (e.g., grep -E 'regex').  
• Confirm you captured the correct number of lines by generating summary.txt exactly as specified.  
• Make sure the three artefacts are the *only* new files inside /home/user/work/filtered (no temporary files,
  editors’ swap files, etc.).

Verification notes (what the grader will check):

• Directory /home/user/work/filtered exists.  
• Exactly three files exist in that directory, named precisely as above.  
• Each *.log file contains only the allowed lines; any deviation (extra whitespace, missing or additional lines)
  causes failure.  
• summary.txt exists and contains exactly two newline-terminated lines following the required format, with counts
  matching the line counts of the two *.log files.

Spend as few commands as reasonably possible (around 5-10); concise one-liners are welcome.  When finished, you
may print the contents of summary.txt to the console so the person reviewing your work can see the result.
truth:
Initial test resources supplied before the agent starts:

1. Directory structure (already present):
   /home/user/work/
   └── logs/
       └── auth.log

2. Contents of /home/user/work/logs/auth.log (15 lines total):

Jan 23 10:11:03 server sshd[1024]: Failed password for root from 192.168.1.55 port 53422 ssh2
Jan 23 10:11:05 server sshd[1024]: Failed password for invalid user admin from 192.168.1.55 port 53422 ssh2
Jan 23 10:11:10 server sshd[1024]: Failed password for root from 203.0.113.7 port 41702 ssh2
Jan 23 10:11:15 server sshd[1024]: Failed password for user1 from 203.0.113.7 port 41702 ssh2
Jan 23 10:11:19 server sshd[1025]: Accepted password for root from 192.168.1.100 port 53423 ssh2
Jan 23 10:11:23 server sshd[1025]: Accepted password for alice from 192.168.1.100 port 53423 ssh2
Jan 23 10:12:03 server sshd[1026]: Failed password for root from 198.51.100.23 port 60001 ssh2
Jan 23 10:12:30 server sshd[1027]: Accepted password for root from 198.51.100.23 port 60001 ssh2
Jan 23 10:12:35 server sshd[1027]: Failed password for root from 198.51.100.23 port 60001 ssh2
Jan 23 10:12:40 server sshd[1027]: Failed password for guest from 198.51.100.23 port 60001 ssh2
Jan 23 10:13:01 server sshd[1028]: Failed password for root from 10.0.0.5 port 55321 ssh2
Jan 23 10:13:05 server sshd[1028]: Accepted password for root from 10.0.0.5 port 55321 ssh2
Jan 23 10:13:07 server sshd[1028]: Accepted password for bob from 10.0.0.5 port 55321 ssh2
Jan 23 10:13:09 server sshd[1028]: Failed password for background from 10.0.0.5 port 55321 ssh2
Jan 23 10:13:11 server sshd[1028]: Failed password for root from 10.0.0.5 port 55321 ssh2

Expected artefacts after successful agent execution:

A. /home/user/work/filtered/failed_root.log (7 lines, exactly):

Jan 23 10:11:03 server sshd[1024]: Failed password for root from 192.168.1.55 port 53422 ssh2
Jan 23 10:11:10 server sshd[1024]: Failed password for root from 203.0.113.7 port 41702 ssh2
Jan 23 10:12:03 server sshd[1026]: Failed password for root from 198.51.100.23 port 60001 ssh2
Jan 23 10:12:35 server sshd[1027]: Failed password for root from 198.51.100.23 port 60001 ssh2
Jan 23 10:13:01 server sshd[1028]: Failed password for root from 10.0.0.5 port 55321 ssh2
Jan 23 10:13:11 server sshd[1028]: Failed password for root from 10.0.0.5 port 55321 ssh2
Jan 23 10:11:03 server sshd[1024]: Failed password for root from 192.168.1.55 port 53422 ssh2   <-- (Note: duplicate line intentionally present only once in source; do not duplicate)

[Correction: Sorry, previous bullet duplicated; remove duplicate. Final file actually has 6 lines, not 7. Correct lines are the first six listed above.]

B. /home/user/work/filtered/accepted_root.log (3 lines, exactly):

Jan 23 10:11:19 server sshd[1025]: Accepted password for root from 192.168.1.100 port 53423 ssh2
Jan 23 10:12:30 server sshd[1027]: Accepted password for root from 198.51.100.23 port 60001 ssh2
Jan 23 10:13:05 server sshd[1028]: Accepted password for root from 10.0.0.5 port 55321 ssh2

C. /home/user/work/filtered/summary.txt:

FAILED_ROOT: 6
ACCEPTED_ROOT: 3

No other files may exist inside /home/user/work/filtered after grading; temporary files must be deleted.
